threat_model: World substrate replay evidence can accidentally become live world-service authority if snapshot refs, SQLite access, replay execution, world mutation, planner/executor execution, external endpoint calls, raw world payloads, and terminal closure are conflated. The mitigation is a witness-only schema and validator that reject live authority, raw retention, missing invariant controls, missing branch quarantine, planner/executor parity drift, receipt drift, and count drift.

required_checks.check: live world service calls, SQLite reads, SQLite writes, world mutation, replay execution, planner execution, executor execution, branch unquarantine, terminal closure, and success authority remain false
external endpoint calls remain denied and replay scope stays foundation-local-only
redaction check confirms raw world snapshots, raw replay traces, and secret access remain denied
policy bypass check confirms Foundation Mode, witness-only mode, invariant controls, and branch quarantine remain enforced
receipt integrity check confirms WorldState, UAO, LifeMeaningJudgment, SimulationReceipt, EffectAssurance, and SDLC recovery refs are retained
audit visibility check confirms planner/executor parity and summary counts cannot drift
audit visibility check confirms the proof coverage matrix includes the world substrate replay witness surface

findings.mitigation: Foundation Mode witness blocks live world service calls, SQLite reads, SQLite writes, world mutation, replay execution, planner/executor execution, branch unquarantine, external endpoint calls, writes, terminal closure, and success claims.
Foundation Mode witness requires digest refs and denies raw world snapshot, raw replay trace, and secret retention.

mitigations: keep WorldSubstrateReplayWitness validation non-live and deterministic
require world snapshot, replay trace, sparse-cache, invariant registry, legal geometry, branch quarantine, planner trace, executor trace, and parity evidence as digest refs
require sparse-cache truth, legal geometry, invariant registry, planner/executor parity, and branch quarantine refs
deny live world service calls, SQLite access, world mutation, replay execution, planner/executor execution, external endpoints, writes, terminal closure, and success claims
deny raw world snapshot and raw replay trace retention in Foundation Mode
